q: Galileo was the first to point out that all motion is relative.
The choice of the frame of reference is arbitrary.
During the 19th century, it was generally believed that light could not travel across empty space.
Ether was an invisible hypothetical material supposed to fill space and through which light waves traveled.
Michelson and Morley found a shift in the fringes of the interference pattern when detecting the motion of the earth through ether.
The Michelson-Morley experiment result is generally considered to be the first strong evidence against the prevalent ether theory.
Einstein’s special theory of relativity is based on two simple postulates.
The laws of physics are the same in all inertial frames of reference.
The speed of light in vacuum is affected by the speed of its source.
An inertial frame is a frame of reference that has no acceleration.
All objects in an inertial frame follow Newton’s first law of motion.
Two events that are simultaneous in one frame of reference may not be simultaneous in another frame moving relative to the first frame.
Time dilation is the phenomenon of time passing more slowly for an observer who is moving relative to another observer.
Each inertial observer determines that all clocks in motion relative to the observer run slower than that observer’s own clock.
Relativistic effects usually become significant when speeds become comparable to the speed of light.
Proper length $L_0$ is the length of an object measured by an observer who is moving relative to the object.
When an object is moving with speed $v$ parallel to its length, its length is measured to be shorter.
Rest mass $m_0$ is measured when a particle is at rest relative to the observer.
Newton’s second law of motion is valid in relativistic mechanics if relativistic momentum $\vec{p}$ is introduced.
A rapidly moving particle increases in mass.
The law of conservation of energy is valid relativistically.
The total energy of an object of mass $m_0$ at rest is $E_0 = m_0 c^2$.
Special Relativity was published in 1905.
General Relativity is a theory of gravity.
Special Relativity applies to observers in non-inertial frames.
